item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
My headcannon is that the Foundation isn't a flawless organization, and it will occasionally have leaks and breaches. I'm sure there are plenty of articles where other organizations have attacked the Foundation on a larger scale (can't find them right now, but I know they exist). If the anarchists wanted to find a location, given enough time and resources, they could.

Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great concept, nice delivery but a small critique:

In the second incident, anarchists use the SCP to attack the foundation. Wouldnt this be considered a breach of secrecy?

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the updoot! Btw, that's not a typo - I figure it would be more likely the anartists would outsource/sponsor anarchists to attack the Foundation than the other way around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. here have a +1 I liked the article

2. doot

3. I believe there's a typo in your special containment procedures, it says "anarchist" where I'm pretty sure you meant "anartist"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty brilliant take on the Horns of Jericho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I removed all those designations after your crit

…whoops, totally slipped my mind there. This is what I get for trying to read four tabs at once. >.>;

Not to offend any clarinets reading this, but every one that I've met has just been a bit… unusual.

I don't know many clarinet players as well as my trumpet player friends, but I do know that reed-smashing ceremonies was a thing, apparently.

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded: Fixed - coincidence you and Zyn pointed that out at the same time?

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
I removed all those designations after your crit, but I'll change the caption to "An example of…" to be more specific. Thanks for that crit and the +1, btw!

And can confirm, trumpet players are pretty good people. Beats the clarinet section, anyway.1

Footnotes
1. Not to offend any clarinets reading this, but every one that I've met has just been a bit… unusual.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
weird mental image of a bunch of gangly teenagers running around in a silly way, playing dumb melodies on a brass band, then holding a G, and laughing like idiots at collapsing something

bwamp bwamp

One of my best friends plays trumpet. My favorite teacher ever also played trumpet. I have a good memory of chill trumpet players.

Nicely done, author! +1 from me here, and I remember this from the forums.

Slight quibble though, the image caption isn't quite accurate, since SCP-3447 is noted as a collection of trumpets, but the image only shows one trumpet. Maybe change it to SCP-3447-1 or the like? So it's more consistent?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the image caption should say "Instance of SCP-3447" or "SCP-3447-insert instance number" instead of just "SCP-3447", since the SCP tag refers to multiple trumpets, not just the one in the image.

Otherwise, +1. Simple and satisfying.

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
Hm, edgy teenagers who like video game music… potential for a GaW-AWCY? crossover? GaWCY?
There's a tale somewhere in there…

Edit: Just bothered to check the GaW hub and noticed that it is in fact a splinter cell of AWCY?. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Glad you enjoyed it, and thanks for pointing that out!

item.title: Re: SCP-3447
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the feedback! Glad it wasn't -10'd in a day.

As for the first note, SCP-████ is, in my mind, something that required D-Class to work together or was musically based, so having a band that presumably trusted each other on-hand to test with made sense.

As for the dollar, 1. The average researcher (and reader) probably doesn't need to know down to a single dollar the amount of damage, so I left it blank for the imagination. and 2. I didn't want to slam my number pad to come up with a random amount. If you think I'm wrong here (wouldn't be surprised if I was), just lemme know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have this weird mental image of a bunch of gangly teenagers running around in a silly way, playing dumb melodies on a brass band, then holding a G, and laughing like idiots at collapsing something.

You easily earn a +1 for provoking that. (Also, they're playing Lanky Kong's song from DK 64 in my head. Bwamp bwamp)

item.title: Re: SCP-3447
item.content:encoded: Pretty good for a first SCP. I would say that you should just write, in the fourth footnote, 'all later perished in testing' rather then saying they were all killed by the same SCP. Also, I think you might as well fully unredact the damages. You've already revealed that it cost about 1.5 million dollars in damages - whats the point of redacting a few irrelevant digits?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite an interesting SCP and the backstory is well thought of. You clearly know quite a lot about the Foundation. Take my +1.

P.S. Typo in third paragraph of description? "During raid" I think should be "During the raid".

item.title: (no title)
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R

item.title: SCP-3447
item.wikidot:authorName: J E S T E R
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP!

Image from [https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Trumpet_1.png] and should be safe for site under Creative Commons Attribution-Share Alike 3.0 Unported License.

Credit to Zyn and Modern_Erasmus for crit and AWCY? support respectively.
